grammar_topics.explanation_en: The passive is used when the focus is on the action or the person/thing affected by the action, rather than who performed it. It is formed using a form of the verb 'be' + the past participle of the main verb.
grammar_topics.explanation_vi: Thể bị động được sử dụng khi sự tập trung nằm ở hành động hoặc người/vật chịu tác động của hành động đó, thay vì người thực hiện hành động. Cấu trúc cơ bản là 'be' + quá khứ phân từ (V3/ed).
rules: Use the passive when the agent is unknown, obvious, or unimportant.
Use it to emphasize new information (usually placed at the end of the sentence).
To avoid starting clauses with long expressions.

examples.sentence: The car was stolen at approximately 1.30 am.
examples.translation: Chiếc xe đã bị trộm vào khoảng 1 giờ 30 sáng.

examples.sentence: The XL500 was designed with young families in mind.
examples.translation: Chiếc XL500 được thiết kế dành cho các gia đình trẻ.

grammar_topics.explanation_en: Common with verbs of reporting or thinking (e.g., believe, say, think). It allows expressing opinions or facts without identifying a specific source.
grammar_topics.explanation_vi: Cấu trúc bị động khách quan, thường dùng với các động từ tường thuật hoặc tư duy. Nó cho phép diễn đạt ý kiến hoặc sự thật mà không cần chỉ rõ nguồn tin cụ thể.
rules: Structure 1: noun + verb in passive form + infinitive / perfect infinitive.
Structure 2: There + verb in passive form + infinitive / perfect infinitive.
Structure 3: It + verb in passive form + that clause.

examples.sentence: Tourism is expected to become a major part of the country's economy.
examples.translation: Du lịch được kỳ vọng sẽ trở thành một phần quan trọng của nền kinh tế đất nước.

examples.sentence: It is thought that the new railway will provide employment opportunities.
examples.translation: Người ta cho rằng tuyến đường sắt mới sẽ cung cấp các cơ hội việc làm.

grammar_topics.explanation_en: The passive is not normally used with continuous perfect tenses. Instead, various prepositional phrases are used.
grammar_topics.explanation_vi: Thể bị động thường không được dùng với các thì hoàn thành tiếp diễn. Thay vào đó, người ta sử dụng các cụm giới từ tương đương.

examples.sentence: The new railway station has been under construction for two years now.
examples.translation: Ga đường sắt mới đã được thi công trong suốt hai năm nay.

grammar_topics.explanation_en: Causative structures describe actions we arrange for others to do or experiences that happen to us.
grammar_topics.explanation_vi: Cấu trúc sai khiến/nhờ vả mô tả các hành động chúng ta sắp xếp cho người khác làm hoặc các trải nghiệm xảy đến với chúng ta.
rules: have/get sth done: actions we arrange for others or negative experiences.
get sb to do: persuading someone.
have sb/sth doing: actions we make somebody/something start doing.

examples.sentence: Did you finally get your bike fixed?
examples.translation: Cuối cùng bạn đã đi sửa xe đạp chưa?

examples.sentence: We'll soon have your car running like new.
examples.translation: Chúng tôi sẽ sớm giúp xe của bạn chạy như mới.

exercises.label: A
exercises.instruction: If the verb in bold is correct, put a tick. If it is incorrect, rewrite it correctly.

items.sentence: Could you tell me where your passport *was been issued*?
When the winning actor was finally announced, a big cheer went up.
items.explanation: Correct use of past simple passive.

items.sentence: Has your business trip to Calcutta next week *confirmed* by the Indian office?
Everyone who *was been invited* to the wedding came.
items.explanation: Past perfect passive is needed for an action before another past action.

items.sentence: The wire coat hanger *invented* in 1903 by a Mr Parkhouse.
items.explanation: Subject is the receiver of the action.

items.sentence: The issue *has discussed* in Parliament yesterday, but no decisions were taken.
By the time we get there, the problem should have been sorted out.
Britain *was be invaded* by the Romans under Emperor Claudius in 43 AD.
The party had been planned months in advance, but we still had problems.
After *be treated*, I was allowed to leave the hospital.
items.explanation: 'After' is followed by the gerund form.

items.sentence: We boarded the ship and were *showing* to our cabin by a steward.
The new supermarket is to be opened by an actor from a soap opera.
items.explanation: Correct future passive with 'is to be'.

exercises.label: B
exercises.instruction: Write a verb from the box in the correct passive form in each gap.

items.sentence: The stolen items ___ in the attic of an empty house last week.
When the open-air cinema opens, the first film ___ will be Vertigo.
Questions ___ in Parliament about the Minister's business interests.
We're looking forward to ___ a guided tour of the whole factory.
Just imagine that when these words ___ , Europeans had not yet discovered America.
Once the barbecue ___ , we'll be able to start cooking.
items.explanation: Present simple passive in a time clause.

items.sentence: Before ___ to see the invention, I had to agree not to tell anyone.
I would really like ___ at the airport, if that's possible.
It ___ very clear to us that we weren't welcome.
Gareth says he's always wanted ___ as someone with a good sense of humour.

exercises.label: C
exercises.instruction: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: Global warming ___ by pollution.
There ___ about 5,400 different species of mammals.
It ___ that gunshots have been heard in the city centre.
items.explanation: Present perfect passive reporting current events.

items.sentence: The escaped prisoner is believed ___ a rope to climb the wall.
It ___ that about 60% of a man's body mass is water.
Over 200 people ___ to have been injured in the explosion.
It is considered impolite in some cultures ___ with your left hand.
items.explanation: Active infinitive needed for the action of eating.

items.sentence: There ___ to have been 60 crates of olives in the order, not 600!
___ has been suggested that people who don't recycle should pay more tax.
Your order is guaranteed ___ within three working days.

exercises.label: D
exercises.instruction: Each sentence contains one verb in the wrong form. Rewrite it correctly.

items.sentence: Heathrow Airport... *has called* the world's busiest...
items.explanation: Present simple passive for a fact.

items.sentence: The land... *sold* to Fairey Aviation...
In 1944... was developed... *was named* after a small village...
terminal building, which *has later renamed* Terminal 2...
passengers could be *transport* from central London...
permission *was been granted* for a fifth terminal...
a new airport... *has been built* on a floating island...

exercises.label: E
exercises.instruction: Complete the sentences using the phrase given.

items.sentence: By the time I take my exams, ___ .
items.answer: I will have been in training as an accountant for a year
items.explanation: Use 'in training' for future perfect continuous meaning.

items.sentence: The redevelopment of the city centre ___ .
items.answer: has been under consideration for some time

items.sentence: Discussions on the issue ___ .
items.answer: have been in progress for over two weeks

items.sentence: The local Art Society's latest works ___ .
items.answer: will be on display at the library this weekend

items.sentence: George's website ___ .
items.answer: has been under construction for a long time

exercises.label: F
exercises.instruction: Rewrite the incorrect causative forms correctly.

items.sentence: We had to have it repaired twice!
we *got delivered it* to our house...
items.explanation: Causative structure is have/get sth done.

items.sentence: have it *check* straight away...
you'd had them do that.
had *my dad to have* a look...
have the company *to come*...
got my brother *to working*...
bet he soon *had running it* smoothly.
get the company *come* out...
get them to give you some compensation.
I'll ___ it to you again. (your teacher / explain)
watching my kitten... soon ___ much better. (me / feel)
Don't bother... ___ it. (a professional / do)
___ was so annoying... (our car / steal)
You won't learn... if you ___ you with your homework. (your parents / help)
the band soon ___ to their hit songs. (us all / dance)
Don't try ___ your dirty work! (me / do)
thought about ___ as short as mine? (your hair / cut)
I ___ me how to juggle... (Karen / show)
problems caused by students ___ by companies. (their essays / write)
I ___ for over 20 minutes on the phone!
Despite ___ , Ashley's always friendly.
The Prime Minister ___ a surprise visit to Syria.
The ___ the manager.
items.answer: staff were provided with extra training by

items.sentence: I think I need ___ me.
The new stadium ___ for much longer...
Do you know why ___ behind after the lesson?
Call Lionel and ___ the reports up.
When Nadine arrived, she soon ___ at all her jokes.
The government must ___ strong measures against crime.
Mrs Davies... would like ___ by Friday.
Joining a gym ___ , but I didn't fancy the idea.
There ___ between 4,000 and 6,000 languages...
I spoke to Victor... he says he ___ while he was on holiday.
Why didn't you ___ you when you moved house?
Profits ___ slightly next year...
It's hard to believe that this film ___ completely by computer.
As soon as Dad ___ , we can leave.

exercises.label: J
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Imagine ___ transported...
...which ___ currently required...
...which has been ___ development...
...air ___ sucked...
...mixed ___ fuel...
...which has to ___ taken...
It has ___ estimated...
the technology ___ been found...
scientists ___ had scramjets perform...
may never ___ successfully developed.
